skill.name: higgsfield-marketplace-cards
skill.description: Generate marketplace-ready product image cards through the Higgsfield CLI, including main listing images, secondary product visuals, and A+ style modules.
skill.summary: Create marketplace listing image sets through the Higgsfield CLI.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer correctly identified real Bash command and network behavior in SKILL.md. Most backtick hits are Markdown formatting, but the installer instruction pipes a remote script into a shell, so publication should wait for safer installation guidance.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped Directly To Shell
high_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs users to run a remote GitHub installer through curl and pipe it directly into sh. This gives the downloaded script immediate shell execution and creates supply-chain and command execution risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pipe-to-shell pattern is explicit on line 26 and is part of the skill workflow. I did not find evidence of malicious intent, but the execution risk is direct.

medium_findings.title: Networked CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and instructs the assistant to run Higgsfield CLI commands that call a backend service and create image jobs. This is expected for the skill, but it executes external commands and sends prompt and image context to a remote backend.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly grants Bash access and gives concrete CLI commands. The risk is contextual because the CLI is central to the skill, but user assets and prompts may leave the local environment.

low_findings.title: Static False Positives In Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are Markdown inline code, asset names, scopes, or output placeholders. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance detections are not supported by the visible SKILL.md lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation text, placeholders, or normal product instructions rather than cryptographic code or reconnaissance commands. No evidence found for those scanner claims.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Installer
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer pattern downloads a script over HTTPS and executes it immediately with sh. Safer guidance should require reviewable installation steps, package pinning, or explicit user-controlled installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in the skill and matches a known high-risk installation pattern.

content.value_statement: Marketplace sellers need complete image sets that match listing goals and platform expectations. This skill routes product details to Higgsfield to create main images, secondary visuals, and A+ style modules.
actual_capabilities: Runs Higgsfield marketplace-cards create commands for listing image generation.
Creates common bundles for main images, product image sets, A+ modules, or full sets.
Accepts product image paths or upload identifiers as generation inputs.
Passes category, product context, brand context, and visual style to the CLI.
Returns labeled image result URLs after jobs complete.
limitations: Requires the Higgsfield CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Uses a remote Higgsfield backend for enhancement and generation.
Does not create generic product photography outside marketplace listing context.
Does not expose internal enhanced prompts unless the user explicitly asks.

use_cases.title: Launch a marketplace listing
use_cases.description: Create a compliant main product image and supporting secondary visuals for a new listing.

use_cases.title: Build A+ content modules
use_cases.description: Generate structured image modules for product detail pages that need benefits, features, and usage visuals.

use_cases.title: Refresh product visuals
use_cases.description: Create updated lifestyle, detail, and infographic images from existing product assets.

prompt_templates.title: Create a main listing image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one marketplace-ready image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a marketplace main image for this product using ./product.jpg. Category: kitchen accessory.

prompt_templates.title: Generate secondary product images
prompt_templates.scenario: Product team needs a main image and supporting listing visuals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create product-images for this insulated bottle using ./bottle.png. Include clean benefits, detail shots, and lifestyle use.

prompt_templates.title: Produce A+ style modules
prompt_templates.scenario: Content manager needs rich product detail page assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create A+ modules for a premium skincare serum using ./serum.jpg. Brand context: clinical, minimal, white and sage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a full marketplace image set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with category, brand, and visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full-set for a sparkling peach lemonade can using ./can.png. Category: beverage. Visual style: bright summer studio with fresh fruit cues.

output_examples.input: Create product images for a peach lemonade can.
output: Main image: labeled marketplace-ready hero image URL.
Infographic: labeled benefit-focused product image URL.
Lifestyle: labeled usage scene image URL.

output_examples.input: Create A+ modules for a skincare serum.
output: A+ hero banner: labeled module image URL.
Features module: labeled product benefit image URL.
How to use module: labeled instruction image URL.

best_practices: Provide a clear product image when available.
Include category, brand context, and visual style for consistent results.
Choose the smallest scope that matches the listing need.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for generic brand photography without marketplace context.
Do not include private customer data in product context.
Do not ask for raw job IDs or internal enhanced prompts unless required.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates marketplace listing visuals, including main product images, secondary product images, and A+ style modules.

faq.question: Does it need the Higgsfield CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI must be installed, available on the PATH, and authenticated.

faq.question: Can it use my product photo?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can pass a local image path or upload identifier to the Higgsfield CLI.

faq.question: Does it generate video or UGC ads?
faq.answer: No. It is limited to marketplace product image cards and listing image sets.

faq.question: Will it show the final prompts?
faq.answer: No. The skill normally returns labeled image URLs and hides backend-enhanced prompts.

faq.question: What security review result applies?
faq.answer: The review found high risk because the skill recommends a remote installer piped directly to a shell.
